title: Writing - Part 2: Exercise 1
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220-260 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt: A sports and leisure company has commissioned a report on the sports and fitness facilities in your local area. Your report should describe what is currently available, assess whether provision meets the needs of different groups in the community, and suggest how it could be improved.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on Sports and Fitness Facilities in Thornton

Introduction
This report was commissioned to assess the current provision of sports and fitness facilities in Thornton and to identify any gaps in service. Data was gathered through community surveys and personal observation.

Current Provision
Thornton currently benefits from one public leisure centre, two grass football pitches managed by the borough council, and a privately operated gym. The leisure centre offers swimming, squash, and a fitness suite. Additionally, several parks contain informal outdoor exercise equipment installed as part of a national health initiative three years ago.

Assessment of Provision
While provision is adequate for young adults, significant gaps exist for other groups. The leisure centre's opening hours are incompatible with the schedules of full-time workers, as it closes at 8 pm on weekdays. Older residents have raised concerns about the absence of low-impact exercise classes, and facilities for children beyond the age of twelve are largely absent. The only private gym in the area is prohibitively expensive for a large proportion of the community.

Recommendations
The following improvements are recommended:
- Extending leisure centre opening hours to 10 pm on weekdays and 9 pm at weekends.
- Introducing subsidised yoga and water aerobics classes targeted at older residents.
- Developing a multi-use games area in Central Park to provide free facilities for teenagers.

Conclusion
With targeted investment, Thornton's sports provision could more equitably serve all age groups and income levels within the community.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt: You recently attended a classical music concert that was described in advance publicity as 'an unmissable world-class experience'. You were deeply disappointed by several aspects of the evening. Write a letter to the concert hall's artistic director explaining what went wrong and suggesting how future events could be better managed.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Mr. Ashworth,

I am writing following my attendance at the performance of Mahler's Ninth Symphony at your venue on the evening of 5th March, an event promoted as 'an unmissable world-class experience'. I regret to say that the reality fell some considerable distance short of this billing, and I feel compelled to share my observations in the hope that they may be of use to your team.

My primary concern relates to the acoustics in the eastern seating block, where I was seated. The sound quality was noticeably inferior to other areas of the hall, with a persistent reverberance that rendered quieter passages unclear. This issue was compounded by insufficient sound insulation from the foyer, through which conversational noise from latecomers was audible during the first movement.

Furthermore, the programme notes distributed on the evening contained several significant factual errors regarding the composition's history, which I found disconcerting for an institution of your standing. While this may appear a minor point, attendees who had no prior familiarity with the work would have been actively misled.

I would respectfully suggest that an acoustic review of the eastern seating sections be commissioned before future performances. I would also recommend that programme notes be verified by a musicologist prior to printing.

Despite these disappointments, I remain a committed supporter of live classical music and sincerely hope that the issues I have raised can be addressed.

Yours sincerely,
Eleanor Forsyth

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt: You have seen the following notice on a community website: 'We are looking for reviews of local fitness classes or sports clubs. Tell us about the activities offered, the atmosphere, and whether you would recommend it to others.' Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
More Than Just Exercise: A Review of Riverside Crossfit

I joined Riverside Crossfit eight months ago with fairly modest ambitions—to get fitter without committing to a soul-destroying solitary gym routine. What I discovered was something far more rewarding than I had anticipated.

The facility itself is unpretentious: a converted warehouse on the edge of the industrial district, equipped with everything necessary and nothing superfluous. The sessions, which run six days a week at various times to suit different schedules, combine strength training, cardio, and gymnastics in constantly varied daily workouts. No two sessions are identical, which eliminates the monotony that I had always found fatal to my previous exercise regimes.

What distinguishes Riverside from any other fitness option I have tried is the community. Members range in age from eighteen to sixty-five and in ability from complete beginners to competitive athletes. Yet the culture that the coaches have cultivated is one of genuine mutual support. Experienced members willingly modify their own workouts to help newer arrivals, and the collective effort of working through a challenging session together creates a powerful sense of camaraderie.

The coaching standard is also consistently high. All three resident coaches hold recognised qualifications and invest considerable time in teaching correct technique to prevent injury.

I would recommend Riverside Crossfit without reservation to anyone seeking a structured, sociable, and genuinely effective approach to fitness. Be warned, however: the first week is humbling. After that, it becomes addictive.
